title: Sold

cover.alt: Book cover showing an adolescent South Asian girl in a head covering against a yellow background.  Above her is the book title 'SOLD'.  At the top of the page in smaller type is 'Patricia McCormick / The Best-Selling Auhor of Cut'.  Below that is a badge saying 'National Book Award finalist'.

overview: This novel is narrated by a 13-year-old Nepali girl sold into sexual slavery in India. She finds ways to educate herself and form bonds with other girls to survive until they are eventually rescued. The book, based on the author's interviews with girls who had been trafficked in India and Nepal, was a finalist for the National Book Award for Young People's Literature.

incidents.description: This was one of 12 library books removed from accessible shelves in the Blackhawk school district in western Pennsylvania, after the school board introduced a policy to remove books, pending review by the board, as soon as a student or parent complained that they were 'not compatible with their values or fundamental religious beliefs'.  {ln:https://www.post-gazette.com/news/education/2023/12/14/blackhawk-school-district-library-books/stories/202312140133|After pushback from local residents, the board restored the previous book challenge policy later in the year}, restoring the book to the shelves. The attempt to remove this book, which was on a widely circulated list of titles adults were urged to challenge, was {ln:https://www.marshall.edu/library/bannedbooks/sold/|not an isolated case}.  In 2026, the American Library Association reported that this book was {ln:https://triblive.com/news/world/book-bans-and-attempted-bans-remain-at-record-highs-with-sold-topping-the-list/|the most challenged book in 2025}.
